natural_sources: Most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere
natural_occurrence.description: An inert, colorless, odorless gas that makes up 78% of Earth's atmosphere. Used as a packaging gas to displace oxygen and prevent oxidation, and as a propellant in aerosol products.

manufacturing.description: Produced industrially by cooling air to liquid state, then fractionally distilling to separate nitrogen from oxygen and other gases. Can also be produced by membrane separation or pressure swing adsorption.

other_uses.industrial: Welding, chemical production, semiconductor manufacturing, cryogenics
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Packaging of medicines, liquid nitrogen for cryotherapy
other_uses.household: Draft beer systems (nitrogen widget), aerosol products

eu.conditions: Authorized as packaging gas and propellant

usa.notes: Approved for direct addition to food for human consumption

japan.notes: Approved as packaging gas and propellant

canada.reference: Lists of Permitted Carrier or Extraction Solvents

safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: Not specified (inert gas, no toxicological concern)

regulatory_history.event: Nitrogen widely adopted for food packaging to prevent oxidation

adi_context.source_note: No ADI needed - nitrogen makes up 78% of the air we breathe. Completely inert and non-toxic.

fun_facts: Makes up 78% of the air you breathe - you inhale it all day
The gas in your potato chip bag that keeps chips crispy
Creates the creamy foam in Guinness beer (nitrogen widget)
Completely inert - doesn't react with food at all
You breathe in about 11,000 liters of nitrogen per day
Liquid nitrogen is -196°C and used for flash freezing food
